# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_broadcast_domain (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Specify the broadcast domain name.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `from_ipspace` (String) if used with `from_name`, it will try to find broadcast domain `from_name` in `from_ipspace`, split action either rename broadcast_domain and ipspace or create a new broadcast domain. If not `from_name` present, it will try to find `name` broadcast domain in `from_ipspace` and modify ipspace using `ipspace`. Only supported with REST.
- `from_name` (String) Specify the broadcast domain name to be split into new broadcast domain.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ipspace` (String) Specify the required ipspace for the broadcast domain. With ZAPI, a domain ipspace cannot be modified after the domain has been created. With REST, a domain ipspace can be modified. This option is required while using REST.
- `mtu` (Number) Specify the required mtu for the broadcast domain.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `ports` (String) Specify the ports associated with this broadcast domain. Should be comma separated. It represents the expected state of a list of ports at any time. Add a port if it is specified in expected state but not in current state. Delete a port if it is specified in current state but not in expected state. For split action, it represents the ports to be split from current broadcast domain and added to the new broadcast domain. If all ports are removed or split from a broadcast domain, the broadcast domain will be deleted automatically. With REST, if exact match of ports found with `from_name`, split action will rename the broadcast domain using `name`. With REST, if partial match of ports with `from_name`, split action will create a new broadcast domain using `name` and move partial matched ports from `from_name` to `name`. With REST, if `ports` not in `from_name`, split action will create a new broadcast domain using `name` with `ports`.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Whether the specified broadcast domain should exist or not.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
